description: Scalar subquery with COUNT(*) in SELECT list returns non-nullable i64

columns.name: order_count
columns.note: COUNT(*) always returns a value (0 if no rows), so non-nullable even as scalar subquery

name: scalar_subquery
query_sql:
-- @name GetUsersWithOrderCount
-- @returns :many
SELECT id, name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE user_id = users.id) AS order_count FROM users
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', bio TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
